seoTitle: How to Log In to Zendesk: Full Sign-In Guide
metaDescription: Sign into Zendesk by locating your subdomain, entering credentials or using SSO, clearing multi-factor checkpoints, and recovering access if you get locked out.
intro: Zendesk logins can trip people up because each organization uses its own subdomain rather than a single universal sign-in page. This guide covers finding your workspace's login link, entering credentials or using single sign-on, clearing multi-factor checkpoints, and recovering access if something goes wrong.

steps.heading: Find your login portal
steps.body: Navigate to the global Zendesk login page and enter your organization's unique subdomain to reach your workspace.

steps.heading: Enter your credentials
steps.body: Type your registered email and password into the standard sign-in fields.

steps.heading: Or use single sign-on
steps.body: If your organization supports it, click the SSO button to log in through Google, Microsoft, or another enterprise identity provider.

steps.heading: Clear multi-factor authentication
steps.body: Complete any required authenticator prompt or device verification step to finish signing in.

steps.heading: Recover a forgotten subdomain or password
steps.body: Use the identity recovery options on the login page if you can't remember your subdomain or credentials.

faq.question: What is a Zendesk subdomain and why do I need it?
faq.answer: It's the unique workspace identifier your organization was assigned, and Zendesk uses it to route you to the correct account instead of a single shared login page.

faq.question: Can I log in with my Google or Microsoft account?
faq.answer: If your organization has single sign-on enabled, you can typically log in through Google, Microsoft, or another supported enterprise identity provider.

faq.question: What do I do if I forgot my Zendesk subdomain?
faq.answer: Use the account recovery options on the login page, which are designed to help you locate your organization's subdomain.

faq.question: Why do I keep getting stuck on the multi-factor authentication step?
faq.answer: This usually means your authenticator app or device isn't syncing correctly, so double-check your device's time settings or try a backup verification method.
